forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+---
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8980'
+title: 'Fix HardeningTests README/SECURITY.md drift: ''### Injected globals'' section moved'
+---
+## What
+`Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolTests/HardeningTests.swift`'s `readmeInjectedGlobalsListMatchesRuntime` test parses a `### Injected globals` section out of the repo root `README.md` (see `HardeningTests.readmeInjectedGlobals()`) and asserts it's set-equal to the runtime-enumerated sandbox globals.
+
+Commit e366c62 ("docs: rewrite README as a library landing page") replaced the M10-hardening-notes README with a library-landing-page README and moved the `## Security model` section (including the `### Injected globals` list) verbatim into `docs/SECURITY.md`, removing it from `README.md`. This broke the machine-checked README<->code sync test: it now fails with `README.md has no "### Injected globals" section.`
+
+Discovered while implementing task 0wf5gad (Add FoundationModelsMetadataRegistry dependency to Package.swift) — confirmed via `git stash -u` that this failure is pre-existing on `main` and unrelated to that task's Package.swift/dependency changes.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `swift test --filter HardeningTests` passes again — either by pointing `HardeningTests.readmeInjectedGlobals()` at `docs/SECURITY.md` (matching where the section actually now lives) or by restoring a `### Injected globals` section to `README.md`, whichever better matches the intended doc structure post-rewrite.
+- [ ] Full `swift test` is green (no regressions elsewhere).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `swift test --filter HardeningTests`
+- [ ] Full `swift test`

+comments:
